title: AgentDoc Patch Apply Result
description: Result of applying a validated adoc.patch.v0 to AgentDoc source via formatting-preserving span splices (working tree only, never Git). Emitted by `adoc patch --apply` and the config-gated `adoc_patch_apply` MCP tool. Refusals (validation failure, source drift, missing placement, disabled MCP gate) are the same envelope with applied:false, empty written_files, and fix-oriented diagnostics — never a protocol error. Exit codes: 0 applied and post-check clean; 1 refused, nothing written; 2 applied but the post-check reports new errors (stop and surface to a human). After a successful apply the graph artifact is stale by design (artifacts_stale:true) — rebuild before further reads. Never auto-reverts. See ROADMAP-V6.md §V6.4, ADR-0036, ADR-0037.

target.description: The patch's target Object ID. Absent for refusals that never reached validation (e.g. the disabled MCP gate or an unparseable patch document).

operation.description: The patch operation as validated. Empty string when validation never ran.

check.description: The embedded adoc.patch.check.v0 envelope (validated against adoc://agent/v0/schema/patch-check.json). Absent for refusals that never reached validation.

written_files.description: Exactly one entry when applied (one patch, one target, one file write); empty for every refusal.

object.description: The target's content_hash before the apply (from the graph artifact; absent for create_object) and after (from the post-check recompile; absent when the post-check could not produce artifacts).

artifacts_stale.description: Always true when applied: apply never rewrites dist artifacts. Run `adoc build` (or adoc_project_status with refresh:"build") before further reads.

proof_obligations.description: Copied from the embedded check: obligations the apply does not discharge (e.g. evidence review for verified-claim changes).

diagnostics.description: Apply-stage refusal reasons (patch.source_drift, patch.create_missing_placement, patch.placement_not_adoc, mcp.patch_apply_disabled) or, when the embedded check failed, a mirror of its diagnostics. Empty on a successful apply — post-check findings live in post_check.diagnostics.

before_file_hash.description: sha256-prefixed hash of the file bytes the splice was planned against; re-verified on disk immediately before the atomic rename (TOCTOU guard).

postCheck.description: The automatic post-apply re-check (full compile from disk). Reported, never acted on: AgentDoc does not auto-revert — the human and Git undo.

proposer.description: The patch's proposer metadata — recorded, not enforced (the permission engine is deferred).
